2604.13016.title: Rethinking On-Policy Distillation of Large Language Models: Phenomenology, Mechanism, and Recipe
2604.13016.paper_summary: On-policy distillation (OPD) has become a core technique in the post-training of large language models, yet its training dynamics remain poorly understood. This paper provides a systematic investigation of OPD dynamics and mechanisms. We first identify that two conditions govern whether OPD succeeds or fails: (i) the student and teacher should share compatible thinking patterns; and (ii) even with consistent thinking patterns and higher scores, the teacher must offer genuinely new capabilities beyond what the student has seen during training. We validate these findings through weak-to-strong reverse distillation, showing that same-family 1.5B and 7B teachers are distributionally indistinguishable from the student's perspective. Probing into the token-level mechanism, we show that successful OPD is characterized by progressive alignment on high-probability tokens at student-visited states, a small shared token set that concentrates most of the probability mass (97%-99%). We further propose two practical strategies to recover failing OPD: off-policy cold start and teacher-aligned prompt selection. Finally, we show that OPD's apparent free lunch of dense token-level reward comes at a cost, raising the question of whether OPD can scale to long-horizon distillation.
2604.13016.paper_summary_zh: 在訓練後的大型語言模型中，基於政策的蒸餾（OPD）已成為一項核心技術，但其訓練動態仍然不甚了解。本文對OPD的動態和機制進行了系統性的研究。我們首先確定了兩個條件決定OPD的成功或失敗：（i）學生和教師應該分享兼容的思維模式；（ii）即使擁有一致的思維模式和更高的分數，教師也必須提供學生在訓練期間未見過的真正新能力。我們通過弱到強的逆蒸餾驗證了這些發現，顯示同家族的1.5B和7B教師在學生的視角下是分佈上無法區分的。深入探討令牌層級的機制，我們顯示成功的OPD特徵在於在學生訪問的狀態中對高概率令牌的漸進對齊，一個小的共享令牌集集中大部分的概率質量（97%-99%）。我們進一步提出了兩種實用策略來恢復失敗的OPD：基於政策的冷啟動和教師對齊的提示選擇。最後，我們顯示OPD在密集令牌層級獎勵中表面上的免費午餐是有代價的，提出了OPD是否能擴展到長期蒸餾的問題。
2604.13016.author: Yaxuan Li et.al.
2604.13016.authors: Yaxuan Li, Yuxin Zuo, Bingxiang He, Jinqian Zhang, Chaojun Xiao, Cheng Qian, Tianyu Yu, Huan-ang Gao, Wenkai Yang, Zhiyuan Liu, Ning Ding
